top.title: AI is already making online crimes easier. It could get much worse.
top.summary: In November, Leonard’s team at Google released a report that found bad actors were using AI tools (including Google’s Gemini) to dynamically alter malware’s behavior; for example, it could self-modify to evade detection. But what set this example apart was that it employed large language models (LLMs). Spam and beyond Attackers started adopting generative AI tools almost immediately after ChatGPT exploded on the scene at the end of 2022.

top.title: What’s next for Chinese open-source AI
top.summary: Just last week the Chinese firm Moonshot AI released its latest open-weight model, Kimi K2.5, which came close to top proprietary systems such as Anthropic’s Claude Opus on some early benchmarks. But these models differ in a crucial way from most US models like ChatGPT or Claude, which you pay to access and can’t inspect. R1 was released as an open-weight model under a permissive MIT license, allowing anyone to download, inspect, and deploy it.

top.title: The Download: AI-enhanced cybercrime, and secure AI assistants
top.summary: These models differ in a crucial way from most US models like ChatGPT or Claude, which you pay to access and can’t inspect. Just as software engineers are using artificial intelligence to help write code and check for bugs, hackers are using these tools to reduce the time and effort required to orchestrate an attack, lowering the barriers for less experienced attackers to try something out. Even when stuck inside the chatbox window, LLMs will make mistakes and behave badly.

top.title: ALS stole this musician’s voice. AI let him sing again.
top.summary: Patrick Darling used a music tool from ElevenLabs to perform a song with his former bandmates. He is also a consultant for ElevenLabs, an AI company that develops agents and audio, speech, video, and music tools. Last month, the company released an album of 11 tracks created using the tool.

top.title: Is a secure AI assistant possible?
top.summary: Building guardrails The term “prompt injection” was coined by the popular LLM blogger Simon Willison in 2022, a couple of months before ChatGPT was released. By Grace Huckins archive page February 11, 2026 Stephanie Arnett/MIT Technology Review | Adobe Stock, Envato EXECUTIVE SUMMARY AI agents are a risky business. Even when stuck inside the chatbox window, LLMs will make mistakes and behave badly.

Modelos.title: Anthropic crece tan deprisa que el problema de OpenAI crece a la misma velocidad: perder el mercado que importa
Modelos.summary: La herramienta no solo ha duplicado usuarios en un mes, sino que ha consolidado la percepción de Claude como "la opción seria" para empresas frente a ChatGPT. Los números explican una paradoja incómoda para OpenAI: ChatGPT procesa 2.500 millones de consultas diarias y arrasa en el mercado de consumo... ...pero Anthropic controla el 32% del mercado empresarial de LLMs según Menlo Ventures, frente al 25% de OpenAI.

Seguridad.title: Hay gente intoxicando la memoria de nuestra IA para manipularnos. Y Microsoft ha encendido todas las alarmas
Seguridad.summary: Hay gente intoxicando la memoria de nuestra IA para manipularnos. No lo decimos nosotros, lo dice el departamento de élite que Microsoft tiene para analizar la seguridad tanto de sus servicios como los de la competencia. En el proceso de una investigación , se han puesto a tirar del hilo y han encontrado que decenas de empresas están insertando instrucciones ocultas en esas funciones de “resumir con IA” con un único objetivo.

Discord faces backlash over age checks after data breach exposed 70,000 IDs
Seguridad.summary: According to Discord, it’s relying on AI technology that verifies age on the user’s device, either by evaluating a user’s facial structure or by comparing a selfie to a government ID. Although government IDs will be checked off-device, the selfie data will never leave the user’s device, Discord emphasized. Both forms of data will be promptly deleted after the user’s age is estimated.

Mercado.title: Pensábamos que la IA nos iba a quitar el puesto. La realidad es que nos está haciendo trabajar más y descansar menos
Mercado.summary: La realidad es que nos está haciendo trabajar más y descansar menos Un estudio analizó durante ocho meses una empresa tecnológica de 200 empleados. La IA aumentó las tareas y la productividad, pero también alargó la jornadas y redujo los descansos voluntarios. Pensábamos que la IA nos iba a quitar el puesto.
